report_name: Self-Propagating "Miasma" Worm Spreads via npm Using "Phantom Gyp" to Steal Credentials

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A threat actor cluster attributed in part to the Miasma worm campaign, leveraging code from the publicly released Mini Shai-Hulud worm.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The Miasma worm spreads by compromising npm packages, a form of supply chain attack targeting software dependencies.

attack_patterns.name: Drive-by Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The Miasma worm executes during the 'npm install' process when a developer installs a compromised package, triggering a malicious script in 'binding.gyp' files.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: The worm abuses trusted relationships by modifying GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows to inject itself into legitimate software build processes, potentially spreading to downstream users.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: The Miasma worm is a credential harvester that scrapes the compromised environment for a wide range of secrets, including npm tokens, GitHub credentials, and cloud service keys.

attack_patterns.name: Unix Shell
attack_patterns.description: The 'Phantom Gyp' technique executes a shell command via the 'action' field in a malicious 'binding.gyp' file to download and run the main payload.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The initial malicious script downloads the Bun JavaScript runtime from an external source to stage and execute the main Miasma payload.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The Miasma worm uses stolen npm tokens and GitHub credentials to authenticate to their respective services and publish new malicious packages or modify CI/CD workflows, thereby propagating itself.

malware.name: Miasma
malware.description: A self-propagating worm spreading through the npm registry. It uses a novel 'Phantom Gyp' technique to execute during package installation and acts as a credential harvester, stealing secrets from developer and CI/CD environments to propagate further.

malware.name: Mini Shai-Hulud
malware.description: A publicly released worm whose code was leveraged by the Miasma worm for its self-propagation capabilities.

tools.name: node-gyp
tools.description: A legitimate Node.js tool for compiling native addon modules, abused by the Miasma worm to execute malicious commands via 'binding.gyp' files during the 'npm install' process.

tools.name: Bun
tools.description: A legitimate JavaScript runtime downloaded and used by the Miasma worm's initial script to execute the main payload, likely to evade detection tools focused on Node.js.

tools.name: curl
tools.description: Used by the malicious 'binding.gyp' action to download the main payload script from an attacker-controlled server.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: The open-source package registry targeted and abused by the Miasma worm for propagation.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: Platform whose credentials and CI/CD workflows (GitHub Actions) are targeted by the Miasma worm for credential theft and propagation.

identities.name: Amazon Web Services
identities.description: Cloud provider whose keys (AWS keys) are targeted for theft by the Miasma worm.

identities.name: Google Cloud Platform
identities.description: Cloud provider whose keys (GCP keys) are targeted for theft by the Miasma worm.

identities.name: Microsoft Azure
identities.description: Cloud provider whose keys (Azure keys) are targeted for theft by the Miasma worm.

identities.name: HashiCorp
identities.description: Vendor whose Vault tokens are targeted for theft by the Miasma worm.

identities.name: Kubernetes
identities.description: Container orchestration platform whose configuration files are targeted for theft by the Miasma worm.

identities.name: Red Hat
identities.description: Referenced in a source article as a victim of the Miasma credential-stealing campaign.

infrastructure.name: Miasma Payload C2
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used to host the Miasma worm's main payload, which is downloaded via a curl command executed from a malicious 'binding.gyp' file.

indicators.name: Miasma Payload Download URL
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Miasma Worm Payload Download
id: 7c8d9e0f-1a2b-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects network requests to a known Miasma worm payload URL seen in 'binding.gyp' files.
references:
  - https://www.stepsecurity.io/blog/binding-gyp-npm-supply-chain-attack-spreads-like-worm
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
tags:
  - attack.t1105
level: critical
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - 'https://evil.com/payload.sh'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects HTTP requests for the Miasma worm payload URL, which is executed via a malicious 'binding.gyp' file during 'npm install'.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of node-gyp
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of node-gyp
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects 'node-gyp' spawning shell or networking utilities, which is indicative of the 'Phantom Gyp' technique used by the Miasma worm.
references:
  - https://www.stepsecurity.io/blog/binding-gyp-npm-supply-chain-attack-spreads-like-worm
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
tags:
  - attack.t1059.004
  - attack.t1195.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\node-gyp.js'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\sh.exe'
      - '\bash.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the 'node-gyp' process spawning shell or networking utilities like 'sh', 'curl', or 'wget'. This behavior is highly indicative of the 'Phantom Gyp' supply chain attack technique.

indicators.name: Anomalous Bun Runtime Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Bun Runtime Execution
id: 6f7a8b9c-0d1e-4f2a-8b3c-4d5e6f7a8b9c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the 'Bun' JavaScript runtime. The Miasma worm downloads and uses this runtime to evade detection. Its presence may be anomalous in environments that do not explicitly use it.
references:
  - https://www.stepsecurity.io/blog/binding-gyp-npm-supply-chain-attack-spreads-like-worm
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
tags:
  - attack.t1105
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\bun.exe'
      - '/bun'
  filter_legit_dev:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\bash.exe'
      - '\zsh'
      - '\vscode.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_dev
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the 'Bun' JavaScript runtime. The Miasma worm downloads and uses Bun to execute its main payload. While Bun is a legitimate tool, its unexpected presence in a CI/CD environment or on a developer machine can be a strong indicator of compromise.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
